examples.title: Creating Tables with Keys and Constraints
examples.description: SQL statements to create tables demonstrating primary key, foreign key, and integrity constraints
examples.code:
CREATE TABLE Department (
    DeptID INT PRIMARY KEY,
    DeptName VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    Location VARCHAR(30) DEFAULT 'Delhi'
);

CREATE TABLE Employee (
    EmpID INT PRIMARY KEY,
    Name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    Age INT CHECK (Age >= 18 AND Age <= 65),
    Salary DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
    DeptID INT,
    FOREIGN KEY (DeptID) REFERENCES Department(DeptID)
        ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE
);
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates: PRIMARY KEY on EmpID and DeptID, NOT NULL constraints, CHECK constraint for domain integrity, DEFAULT values, and FOREIGN KEY with referential actions.

examples.title: Python Class Representing Relation
examples.description: Object-oriented representation of a relation with key operations
examples.explanation: This demonstrates Entity Integrity (checking NULL PK), uniqueness constraint, and basic relation structure. The class enforces rules before insertion.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for insert due to duplicate check

examples.title: Relational Algebra - Key Selection Operations
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating key operations in relational algebra
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FindPrimaryKeys
BEGIN
    INPUT: Relation R with set of attributes A
    OUTPUT: Set of candidate keys
    
    candidates = empty set
    
    // Step 1: Find Super Keys
    superkeys = []
    FOR each subset S of A DO
        IF S uniquely identifies all tuples THEN
            Add S to superkeys
        END IF
    END FOR
    
    // Step 2: Find Minimal Super Keys (Candidate Keys)
    FOR each superkey SK in superkeys DO
        isMinimal = TRUE
        FOR each proper subset SS of SK DO
            IF SS in superkeys THEN
                isMinimal = FALSE
                BREAK
            END IF
        END FOR
        IF isMinimal THEN
            Add SK to candidates
        END IF
    END FOR
    
    RETURN candidates
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm finds all candidate keys by first identifying all superkeys, then filtering to find minimal ones. Candidate keys are minimal superkeys.
examples.timeComplexity: O(2^n) where n is number of attributes

examples.title: C Structure for Relation with Integrity Checks
examples.description: C program demonstrating relation structure and integrity enforcement
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates Entity Integrity (checking PK is not zero), Domain Constraints (validating age range, name not empty), and Uniqueness Constraint (checking for duplicate primary keys).
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for insert due to duplicate check
